observations.recommendation: We recommend that management direct the Property Officer together with the Disposal Committee to process the documents/forms for disposal of unserviceable properties and submit the same to the COA Auditor in Charge pursuant to the Handbook on Property and Supply Management System, and Section 79 of PD No. 1445.

We recommend that management comply with the provisions of Section 287 of the Local Government Code of 1991 and the related DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2020-1 on the full utilization of the 20% Development Fund for the implementation of priority development projects in order to optimally attain the socio-economic and environmental development of the municipality.  Henceforth, utilize the balances of the 20% Development Fund continuing appropriations for the implementation of development projects in order to attain the intended purpose for which the fund was appropriated.

We recommend that management provide resources for the revision/updating of the Local Revenue Code in accordance with Republic Act 7160 or the Local Government Code of 1991 and coordinate with the DILG, DOF and BLGF in the proper imposition of taxes and fees.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year which data should come from the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR) or Taxpayer’s index card maintained at the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and furnish the Municipal Accountant copy of the list to serve as the basis in recording the Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable accounts in the books.  This is in view of the need to record in the books not mere estimates from real property taxes but actual receivables from said taxes pursuant to Section 19 and 20 of the NGAS Manual, Volume 1 in order to reflect in the books accurate amounts for the RPT and SET Receivables.
We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit the collections daily or not later than the next banking day as required under Section 32 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.

We recommend the management to transfer the completed projects booked under CIP accounts to their appropriate PPE accounts and take up the corresponding depreciation for the current and previous years in accordance with PPSAS 17.
observations.managementAction: Adjustments was made to closed the CIP account thru JEV Nos. 02-2519, 02-2520, 01-2515, 01-2516,01-2599, 01-0019, 05-0060, 01-0020, 01-3370, 01-0036.  Depreciation was taken up thru JEV Nos. 01-1821, 04-3471, 05-4431.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the concerned department/personnel in-charge properly review the taxes withheld to ensure correctness of BIR remittances to avoid penalties.
observations.managementAction: Expanded Withholding Tax (EWT) and Value Added Tax (VAT) tax base computation were corrected to reflect the accurate base amount for the deductions.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to submit an explanation on the deficiencies noted and require the concerned officers to complete all the related information and supporting documents for the distribution of relief goods in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD 144, otherwise a Notice of Disallowance shall be issued on the deficiencies noted.
observations.managementAction: The OSCA Head and MSWDO on March 4, 2021 submitted the details of the 11 unknown recipients; the authorization letter of those senior citizens whose authorization were not attached on the list and other deficiencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the agency’s Information Technology Officer (If there are any) or make queries with the etracs system’s developer to troubleshoot the problem immediately.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer resort to Manual entry of the Accountable Forms Inventory pending updates of the LGU’s etracs system.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management prepare and update LDRRMFIP for the DRRM program annually according to the prescribed format required under Annex A of COA Circular 2012-002. Also, we recommend that management completely assign personnel to conduct DRRM activities in consonance with Section 2, Rule 6 of the IRR of RA 10121.
observations.managementAction: Prepared the LDRRMFIP according to prescribed format.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to comply with the requirements of Section 53(e) of RA 9184 and Section 4 of the Administrative Code No. 17, series of 2011 as well as paragraph 1.1 of DBM Circular Letter No. 2011-6 in the procurement of Common-Use Supplies from PS-DBM. If the items to procure are not physically available or falls under the conditions that it is inefficient, impractical and uneconomical, then the Certificate of Non-Availability of Stocks (CNAS) must be attached.
observations.managementAction: Already sent Agency Procurement Request to PS-DBM on March 24, 2020 and PS-DBM already received the Price Quotation and Stock Availability Certification on March 25, 2020 but was not push through due to COVID 19 pandemic. Unavailability of transportation in going to Cebu City due to COVID 19.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Municipal Accountant the certified list of taxpayers showing the amount due and collectible for the year so that the RPT and SET Receivables can be adjusted by the Municipal Accountant to reflect the correct balances at year-end. The list should have been prepared at the beginning of the year to provide a reasonable basis in setting up the RPT receivables for the year.
observations.managementAction: The difference found between the RPTAR and SET Receivables outstanding balances was adjusted per JEV-2021-01-001373.  However, the certified list of taxpayers showing the amount due and collectible for the year of the RPT and SET Receivables was not prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to submit the Titles with Annotations on the Deed of Absolute Sale for the above transactions and pay the necessary taxes that should be shouldered by the agency as prescribed in the above-stated law.
observations.managementAction: A staff was assigned to processed the annotation with concerned government agencies i.e. BIR, ROD.  Subsequent payment of RROW acquisition already follow the guidelines.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management call the attention of the different Agency Heads and Accountable Officer of the recipient fund transfers and cash advances, respectively, and immediately require them to liquidate the long outstanding funds for projects implemented or travel completed. Otherwise, require them to return the funds that were not utilized.
observations.managementAction: Payments/Liquidations were received.  A remaining balance amounting to P135,600.00 and P7,215.49 were still waiting for the complete submission of documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to review the reasons behind the contractor’s failure to accomplish the said projects within the scheduled time and submit justification letter. Further, we recommend that management act on the appropriate measures in the non-completion of the mentioned projects such as but not limited to forfeiture of performance security, imposition of liquidated damages or termination of contract as warranted.
observations.managementAction:
Project #1 (Concreting of Road) – 
100% complete for both financial and physical aspect;
To call the attention of YS Construction to claim the remaining 10% accomplishment undisbursed

Project #2 (Construction of Multi-purpose Bldg.) – 
Show Cause Letter issued on Jan.31, 2020 & duly received by contractor on Feb. 3, 2020;
Contract Terminated  last Mar. 2020;
Contract of the remaining works successfully awarded to 3N construction, the 5th bidder

Project # 3 (Repair of CPG Multi-purpose) – 
100% completed last Oct.30, 2020 subject for Liquidated Damages

Project #4 (Rehabilitation of Magsaysay Access Road) – 
Final inspection conducted August 4, 2020

Project # 5 (Upgrading of Municipal Roads) – 
Negotiation of lot owners subject to Road Right of Way was successfully conducted together with the Provincial Legal Office;
Project implementation resumed last December 28, 2020;
Plan implementation was delayed due to the Pandemic situation and the most of the Heirs Vicente Lao Jr. the lot owner are residing in U.S.A

Project #6 (Trading Post/Bagsakan) – 
Project completed on March 2020 and already operational contractor subjected for liquidated damages

Project #7 (Construction of Evacuation Center) – 
100% completed on January 15, 2020 and the project was utilized as Quarantine Facility of the MLGU

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management prepare and update LDRRMFIP for the DRRM program annually according to the prescribed format required under Annex A of COA Circular 2012-002. Also, we recommend that management completely assign personnel to conduct DRRM activities in consonance with Section 2, Rule 6 of the IRR of RA 10121.
observations.managementAction: As discussed with the Municipal Mayor together with the HRMO durinf exit conference regarding the hiring of staff in charge for operations and warnings. The vacant position was already posted for possible hiring but the Municipal Mayor decided to delay due to the COVID -19 Pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that GAD Focal Point System regularly assess the status of implementation of GAD Plan and Budget to timely and properly evaluate those activities that are not yet fully implemented before the calendar year ends, in order to fully maximize GAD funds and provide new or better services to the intended beneficiaries pursuant to the provisions of DBM-NEDA-NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-1.
observations.managementAction:
LGU personnel under the MSWD has yet to conduct training on GAD database management system formulation or outsource the formulation from experts.

A quarterly review, assessment and monitoring was conducted to keep track of the progress made on the implementation of the PPA’S under GAD. Each department with GAD related PPA’s was constantly monitored by the GAD Focal Person still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Accountant to create special accounts for Talibon Polytechnic College and Talibon Integrated Transport Terminal in the e-NGAS system in order to provide separate financial information such as subsidiary ledgers, trial balances and financial statements as required under the afore-stated provisions of GAM, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Special account was not yet created due to constraint brought by the COVID 19 pandemic and typhoon Odette.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to compute and collect liquidated damages due from the contractor and submit the required supporting documents to support the variation order in consonance with Annex E of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The management has yet to process for the recommended implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Head of the Agency ensure that settlement of the disallowances/charges is made.
observations.managementAction: The settlement was not processed due interruptions brought by COVID 19 activities and restrictions.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Head of the Agency ensure that settlement of the disallowances/charges is made.
observations.managementAction: To apply for relief of accountability. The plan was dropped due to interrupted work schedule caused by COVID 19 pandemic

The concerned barangays already submitted to the Auditor in charge the liquidation reports but was returned due to lacking documents. Constant follow-up has been made since.

Also, we recommend that management completely assign personnel to conduct DRRM activities in consonance with Section 2, Rule 6 of the IRR of RA 10121.

Further, we recommend that management act on the appropriate measures in the non-completion of the mentioned projects such as but not limited to forfeiture of performance security, imposition of liquidated damages or termination of contract as warranted.

We recommend that GAD Focal Point System regularly assess the status of implementation of GAD Plan and Budget to timely and properly evaluate those activities that are not yet fully implemented before the calendar year ends, in order to fully maximize GAD funds and provide new or better services to the intended beneficiaries pursuant to the provisions of DBM-NEDA-NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-1.

We recommend that GAD Focal Point System regularly assess the status of implementation of GAD Plan and Budget to timely and properly evaluate those activities that are not yet fully implemented before the calendar year ends, in order to fully maximize GAD funds and provide new or better services to the intended beneficiaries pursuant to the provisions of DBM-NEDA-NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-1.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant provide annual depreciation on all depreciable assets based on available pertinent property and accounting records which shall be computed based on the estimated useful life of the asset using the straight-line method, pursuant to Section 4(o) of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs. Accordingly, the following journal entries shall be made by the accountant:

To recognize depreciation in CY 2019 for depreciable assets totaling P2,306,922.53 purchased during CY 2018:

DR – Prior Year’s Adjustment
Cr – Accumulated Depreciation – Name of Asset
observations.managementAction: Depreciation for Other Land Improvements and Buildings was not made since these were transferred to the recipient barangays and NGA’s via an SB Res. No. 2019-295 dated Oct. 28, 2019. No depreciation adjustment was made on Other Structures – Sanitary Toilet since the start of its depreciation was on January 2019. The adjustment for depreciation was only made to Other Property Plant and Equipment per JEV 2019-01-7490.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Accountant/concerned personnel to verify and analyze the factors contributing the occurrence of the negative balances. If appropriate adjustments should be done in the books to effect the necessary corrections, submit the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to the Commission on Audit with support of the proper documents.
observations.managementAction: The negative balance of Due to GSIS was already paid per OR # 4891739 dated Dec. 28, 2018 but posted in Jan. 2019. Due to BIR was already offset on Jan. 2019 upon the refund of over-withheld tax to employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Accountant to submit an explanation why these properties remained in the Trust Fund despite completion. We further recommend the immediate transfer of all completed property, plant and equipment to the General Fund upon liquidation to the grantor pursuant to Section 104 of NGAS, Volume 1.
observations.managementAction:
The amount of P285,000.00 was already transferred to Gen. Fund per JEV 2019-01-000192 dated Jan, 2019 while 
P3,786,010.41 representing School Building at Santo Nino High School was already  transferred to the General Fund per JEV-2019-08-387.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Management to require the Municipal Accountant to trace the transactions and reconcile errors and variances that affect the reliability of the recorded Due from Other Funds and Due To Other Funds accounts and make the necessary corrections/adjustments.
observations.managementAction: JEV 2019-01-1812 was drawn to correct the difference.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management exert extra efforts in reconciling the balances per physical count report and the accounting records and prepare adjusting entries to present accurate balance of the accounts. Also, to ensure that all receipts and issuance of supplies are accounted, and recorded and verify if they were unauthorized issuances of supplies otherwise, it has to be investigated and appropriate sanction be imposed.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting entries were already made to correct the differences per JEV no. 2019-01-1784 and 1785.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe Section 48 of the General Appropriations Act (GAA) for Fiscal Year 2018 in preparing the monthly payroll to ensure that net take home pay must not be reduced below the required minimum pay.
observations.managementAction: The payroll maker was already called up about this matter but she finds it hard to implement considering that the employees involved has lower Salary Grade.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend to management that –

1. Collecting Officers have to turn over all their collections particularly RPT intact daily or not later than the next working day to the Liquidating Officer pursuant to Section 31, Volume I of the NGAS Manual.

2. Install, implement and monitor other necessary internal controls to safeguard government funds.
observations.managementAction: Meeting with collectors was conducted to remind them of their responsibility to turn over collections daily to avoid misuse of funds.  IAS also monitor the remittances of each collector.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that all financial assistance and all payments be supported with necessary documents.
observations.managementAction: The implementing department DA and Engineering Office were instructed to comply with the guidelines and thus the Municipal Engineer attended one of the sessions of the Sangguniang Bayan. A generic resolution was passed giving authority to the Mayor to sign MOA for fund transfers.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the LGU administrator, treasury and accounting offices to adhere to COA Circular 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 to properly account and control gasoline, oil and lubricants consumption and use of motor vehicles. Moreover, we recommend that the LGU submit the monthly fuel consumption reports, monthly report official travels and properly filled out trip tickets to the auditor for verification purposes to determine the reasonableness of fuel consumed during the period.
observations.managementAction: Instructions were made to the Clerk in charge to follow the recommendation for the year 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that GAD Focal Point System regularly assess the status of implementation of GAD Plan and Budget to timely and properly evaluate those activities yet for implementation before the calendar year ends, in order to fully maximize GAD funds and provide new or better services to the intended beneficiaries pursuant to the provisions of DBM-NEDA-NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-1.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Person was called why there was a low utilization on the GAD Programs. She explained that some of the allocations were not utilized because the activities were conducted and funded by the Provincial Government and National Government Agencies. Also, some activities were shortened to minimize the cost but still achieving the same result.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund so that all its programmed development projects could be implemented immediately to ensure that intended beneficiaries’ avail of the maximum benefits that will be derived therefrom.
observations.managementAction: Unimplemented funds for the different programs and projects were implemented during CY 2018 and 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the concerned LGU Officials coordinate with the supplier to rectify these obvious defects/deficiencies.
observations.managementAction: The office of the Mun. Engineer coordinated with the deficiencies including the training of LGU personnel to operate and maintain the equipment.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management ensure that liquidation of the financial assistance granted to LGUs be made upon completion of the project or the intended purpose has been served in accordance with existing regulations and to refrain from extending grant to recipients with unliquidated financial assistance.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were sent to the different barangays on March 8, 2019 requesting for liquidation and or fast tract the implementation of the projects and thereafter submit liquidation report. As of to date partial liquidation of P2,772,992.25 is already recorded in the books.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Property Officer initiate the processing of disposing the unserviceable properties in accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445 and rules and procedures provided in the Handbook on Property and Supply Management System.
observations.managementAction: The GSO had prepared the necessary documents for the disposal and request for disposal was already submitted to COA for inspection.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to observe timely liquidation of cash advances for local travels in compliance with Item 5.1.3 of COA Circular 97-002, dated February 10, 1997 to avoid suspension and withholding of their salaries.
observations.managementAction: During one of the Executive meetings this was taken up. The Mayor reminded on the importance to submit liquidation on cash advances in order that salaries of concern employees will not be withheld. The Municipal Accountant is reminded to monitor on the liquidation of cash advances. As of to-date P238,369.59 is already liquidated thus leaving a balance of P9,570.00 of Alex de la Cerna, a Police Officer previously assigned in the municipality. We are still trying to verify his whereabouts for us to send demand letter.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management ensure that liquidation of the financial assistance granted to LGUs be made upon completion of the project or the intended purpose has been served in accordance with existing regulations and to refrain from extending grant to recipients with unliquidated financial assistance.

We recommend that the Property Officer initiate the processing of disposing the unserviceable properties in accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445 and rules and procedures provided in the Handbook on Property and Supply Management System.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant provide annual depreciation on all depreciable assets based on available pertinent property and accounting records which shall be computed based on the estimated useful life of the asset using the straight-line method, pursuant to Section 4(o) of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs. Accordingly, the following journal entries shall be made by the accountant:
To recognize depreciation in CY 2019 for depreciable assets totaling P2,306,922.53 purchased during CY 2018:

We recommend the Municipal Accountant/concerned personnel to verify and analyze the factors contributing the occurrence of the negative balances. If appropriate adjustments should be done in the books to effect the necessary corrections, submit the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to the Commission on Audit with support of the proper documents.

We recommend the Municipal Accountant to submit an explanation why these properties remained in the Trust Fund despite completion. We further recommend the immediate transfer of all completed property, plant and equipment to the General Fund upon liquidation to the grantor pursuant to Section 104 of NGAS, Volume 1.

We recommend the Management to require the Municipal Accountant to trace the transactions and reconcile errors and variances that affect the reliability of the recorded Due From Other Funds and Due To Other Funds accounts and make the necessary corrections/adjustments.

We recommend that management exert extra efforts in reconciling the balances per physical count report and the accounting records and prepare adjusting entries to present accurate balance of the accounts. Also, to ensure that all receipts and issuance of supplies are accounted, and recorded and verify if they were unauthorized issuances of supplies otherwise, it has to be investigated and appropriate sanction be imposed.
We recommend that the Head of the Agency ensure that settlement of the disallowances/charges is made, otherwise, appropriate actions has to be enforced.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe Section 48 of the General Appropriations Act (GAA) for Fiscal Year 2018 in preparing the monthly payroll to ensure that net take home pay must not be reduced below the required minimum pay.

We recommend the management to observe timely liquidation of cash advances for local travels in compliance with Item 5.1.3 of COA Circular 97-002, dated February 10, 1997 to avoid suspension and withholding of their salaries.

We recommend to management that –
1. Collecting Officers have to turn over all their collections particularly RPT intact daily or not later than the next working day to the Liquidating Officer pursuant to Section 31, Volume I of the NGAS Manual.
2. Install, implement and monitor other necessary internal controls to safeguard government funds.
We recommend that all financial assistance and all payments be supported with necessary documents.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the LGU administrator, treasury and accounting offices to adhere to COA Circular 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 to properly account and control gasoline, oil and lubricants consumption and use of motor vehicles. Moreover, we recommend that the LGU submit the monthly fuel consumption reports, monthly report official travels and properly filled out trip tickets to the auditor for verification purposes to determine the reasonableness of fuel consumed during the period.

We recommend that GAD Focal Point System regularly assess the status of implementation of GAD Plan and Budget to timely and properly evaluate those activities yet for implementation before the calendar year ends, in order to fully maximize GAD funds and provide new or better services to the intended beneficiaries pursuant to the provisions of DBM-NEDA-NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-1.

We recommend the management to require all concerned municipal officers and employees to immediately settle their outstanding cash advances by issuing demand letters. Otherwise, we recommend that management withhold or suspend salaries of officials and employees for non-compliance therewith pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: During one of the meeting of the Department Heads the Municipal Accountant discussed these findings and it was agreed that all liquidation cash advances shall be strictly monitored by the accounting department

observations.recommendation: Direct the Property Officer to process the application for disposal of unserviceable properties to the COA Auditor supported by the forms pursuant to the Handbook on Property and Supply Management System, and Section 79 of PD No. 1445. Upon disposal, drop the cost of the PPE as of the year end.
observations.managementAction: Submit application for disposal of unserviceable properties at COA, Tagb. City, sometime in June 2018 for inspection. These properties were already sold thru public auction on November 23, 2018 per OR #4887394

observations.recommendation: We recommend the accounting office to continue the verification and analysis of the factors contributing the occurrence of the negative balances. If appropriate adjustments should be done in the books to effect the necessary corrections, submit the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to the Commission on Audit with support of the proper documents.
observations.managementAction: Verification and reconciliation of the SL of these accounts were done by the payroll in charge and the Journal Entries (JEV) were prepared to correct the balances. Although there are four (4) with over remittance, the Mun. Accountant already talked to them and they promise to refund the over remitted amount.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management effect the immediate submission of liquidation reports to the concerned source agencies and remit the remaining balances of the funds after the completion of the project unless otherwise stipulated in the Memorandum of Agreement that unused funds shall no longer be required to be returned to the source agency.
observations.managementAction: The municipal Mayor instructed the implementing departments to fastract the implementation of funds downloaded from NGA’s. Likewise periodic Liquidation Report is being prepared by the Accounting Department for submission to the concerned agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management immediately stop the practice of charging to SEF transactions outside the scope for which this fund was established and observe the proper utilization thereof pursuant to the provisions of DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular Nos. 1, series of 2017 to accomplish the government’s goal in the operation and maintenance of elementary and secondary schools.
observations.managementAction: Upon receipt of the AOM, the Mun. Mayor called the attention of the Mun. Budget Officer and Mun. Accountant that expenses of this kind will no longer be allowed.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to immediately send demand letters on all the delinquent stall holders as of December 31, 2017 then proceed to the remedies by administrative or judicial action as enumerated in section 174 of RA 7160 if payments of rents are still not recovered.
observations.managementAction: Send demand letters to delinquent stall holders.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to ascertain whether or not these payable accounts are still due for remittance to concerned offices.  If these are still due for remittance, instruct the Municipal Treasurer to remit immediately the amount involved in order to protect the interest of the employee-members concerned and clear the books of these prior years’ payable accounts.
observations.managementAction: The amount represents Dec. 2017 deductions from employees. These were remitted on Jan. 31, 2018 per DV #100-18-01-244

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LGU allocate 1% of its IRA for the implementation of LCPC-related programs, projects and activities in accordance with Section 15 of RA 9344. Further, the LGU need to prepare its LCPC Plan based on the applicable PAPs enumerated in DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 annually and ensure that the amount appropriated for LCPC is fully utilized during the year it is appropriated.
observations.managementAction: The recommendation was taken up during one of the Executive meetings. The LCE instructed the MSWDO and Budget Officer to adher the provisions of the DILF Memo No. 2012-120 in the ensuing year.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Agency Head to determine a suitable manner of paying the salaries and wages of the officials and employees of the municipality in consonance with Item 4.1.2 and 4.1.3 of COA Circular 90-331, dated May 3, 1990 and Sec. 101 of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Stop the practice.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that financial assistance and all payments be supported with necessary documents. In the case of fund transfer as financial assistance, we require that MOA between LCE and the barangay Captain be attached to ensure proper fund utilization and to facilitate fund monitoring.
observations.managementAction: Upon receipt of the AOM, the Mun. Mayor instructed those Department Heads concerned in the implementation to effect immediately the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that GAD Focal Point System regularly assess the status of implementation of GAD Plan and Budget to timely and properly evaluate those activities yet for implementation before the calendar year ends, in order to fully maximize GAD funds and provide new or better services to the intended beneficiaries pursuant to the provisions of DBM-NEDA-NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-1.
observations.managementAction: GAD Focal Point System will regularly assess the status of implementation of GAD Plan and Budget for CY 2018. It was decided that the remaining GAD Programs that was not implemented for CY 2017 will be incorporated in GAD Plan for CY 2018. Some will be replaced with new services.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the COA Audit Team on or before the 15th day after the end of each month in adherence to COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already submitted the DRRM Reports every month for CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund so that all its programmed development projects could be implemented immediately to ensure that intended beneficiaries’ avail of the maximum benefits that will be derived therefrom.
observations.managementAction: During one of the Executive meetings, the Mun. Mayor asked for the list of priority projects funded under the 20% Dev’t Fun. On the top are counterpart funds for NGA’s and barangay projects as well as livelihood components for the accredited PO’s. The implementing departments were likewise instructed to fastract project implementation so that funds will not lay idle in the deposit bank.
observations.reason: Counterpart funds for projects especially from the NGA’s were not yet downloaded hence the utilization thereof is also affected. For livelihood projects, the accredited POs also has a very slow turn-out of Project Proposal.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the concerned LGU Officials coordinate with the supplier to rectify these obvious defects/deficiencies.
observations.managementAction: The office of the Mun. Engineer coordinate with the deficiencies including the training of LGU personnel to operate and maintain the equipment.

Otherwise, we recommend that management withhold or suspend salaries of officials and employees for non-compliance therewith pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD 1445.

If these are still due for remittance, instruct the Municipal Treasurer to remit immediately the amount involved in order to protect the interest of the employee-members concerned and clear the books of these prior years’ payable accounts.

Further, the LGU need to prepare its LCPC Plan based on the applicable PAPs enumerated in DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 annually and ensure that the amount appropriated for LCPC is fully utilized during the year it is appropriated.

We recommend that GAD Focal Point System regularly assess the status of implementation of GAD Plan and Budget to timely and properly evaluate those activities yet for implementation before the calendar year ends, in order to fully maximize GAD funds and provide new or better services to the intended beneficiaries pursuant to the provisions of DBM-NEDA-NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-1.

We recommend that the concerned LGU Officials coordinate with the supplier to rectify these obvious defects/deficiencies.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the COA Audit Team on or before the 15th day after the end of each month in adherence to COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
We recommend that management require the Inventory Committee to adhere to the provisions of the NGAS Manual.
observations.managementAction: The inventory report was submitted on March 3, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management use their available resources first for the implementation of projects instead of availing loans to avoid the incurrence of interest expenses in excess of interest income.
observations.managementAction: The meeting with the Finance committee was conducted and they come up with the decision that the last tranche of the loan with LBP will no longer be availed of and realign the continuing appropriations no longer needed.
observations.reason:
The last tranche of the loan with Land Bank of the Phil. was no longer availed of. Balances of continuing appropriations were realigned to pay the contractor of the 2nd floor of the commercial building 1.  
The  time deposit of P51,273,346.67 as of Dec. 31, 2016 are already reserved for continuing appropriations/ trust liabilities/projects whose implementation are ongoing and to be used for payment when billed for their accomplishments.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Accountant to subsequently include duly verified/accepted accomplishment reports in the checklist of supporting documents for the payrolls of job orders/casual workers in consonance with Section 4(6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445 & COA Circular No. 2012 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: A meeting was conducted with the dept. heads and during the meeting it was unanimously agreed that the payrolls charged to project will be required to attach accomplishment reports. Those Job Orders assigned in offices whose works are routinely done are no longer required to attach accomplishment reports.
observations.reason: The paper works involved in the preparation of accomplishments reports will be time consuming and will cause delay in the processing of payrolls. The mere fact that the supervisor signed the DTR is already a manifestation that the concerned Job Order has done their work assignments.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Section 52 of the General Appropriations Act (GAA) for Fiscal Year 2016 be faithfully adhered to. Management must make sure that its employees will receive the minimum monthly net take home pay of P3,500.00 for them to maintain self-respect and dignity as government employees.

We further recommend the management to explain why they persistently allow the municipality to become a collecting agent for private lending institutions for loan payments deducted from the payrolls on the salaries of its employees even if it is explicitly stated that it is not allowed by law as provided in the above provisions. Payment of these loans should be made individually by the employees concerned to these lending institutions.
observations.managementAction: Memorandum No. 17-03 dated January 17, 2017 was issued to all signing officials to refrain from signing loan documents to employees whose net take home falls below the requirements per GAA 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that a full depth reconstruction of the portion of the road pavement that manifested scaling/raveling shall be made or deduct from the contract price the amount of P440,953.94.
observations.managementAction: The Mayor, Mun. Engineer and the contractor Mai Construction and PQ Equipment held a meeting and discussed the auditor’s findings. It was agreed that they will correct the deficiencies the soonest possible time otherwise the contract of the Integrated Terminal with Mai Construction will no longer be awarded to them.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management plan the Local School Board Budget for Special Education Fund (SEF) ahead of the deadline set in RA 5447.
observations.managementAction: During the meeting with the Local School Board the Mayor enjoined the LSB Members to strictly adhere the guidelines of RA 5447 and instruction was given to the LSB Secretary to calendar the budget preparation and hearing so that he can arrange his schedule.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) submit an explanation as to the delay of the supplier in posting the Performance Security. Likewise, we recommend the BAC to follow the necessary posting requirements of Performance Security pursuant to Section 39.1 of RA 9184 in order to guarantee the faithful performance by the winning bidder of its obligations under the contract in accordance with its Bidding Documents.
observations.managementAction: Upon receipt of the AOM the BAC Chairman instructed the BAC Secretary and the Procurement Office/GSO to strictly follow the posting of performance bond by contractors/suppliers.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to charge its appropriate expense item in the annual budget.

Require the Municipal Accountant to record the wages of casual employees to its appropriate account – Salaries and Wages-Casual as prescribed in the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Chart of Accounts. 

Maximize the services of permanent employees so as not to exceed the 45 % Personal Services limitation.
4) Stop this practice immediately.
observations.managementAction: A meeting with the Local Finance Committee was done. It was decided that the accounting entry for the Job order payroll would still be under MOOE since the budget is taken from MOOE. If it will be taken up as PS the more that the LGU can no longer hire regular employees since it will exceed the PS limitation.
observations.reason: Wages of casual employees are already recorded as Salaries and Wages-Casual while Job Orders are retained as MOOE. The Job Orders have no employee-employer relationship thus they are charged to MOOE. Also, the budgets for the Job Orders are also under MOOE.
